_comment: Pre-written answers the Tier 3 live driver feeds to the workflow so no person is needed. The pitch and roles come from frontend/docs/PrototypeBriefV2.md. Two answers are yours to confirm — they are marked NEEDS_CONFIRMATION. Nothing here runs until Part 2 (the live driver) is wired.

intake.pitch: A Transaction Import & Approval System. Importers upload files of transactions and review them; Approvers review, approve or reject, and export transactions. Files drive ingestion (File Logs); transactions move through Imported / Approved / Rejected.

signIn.rationale: The backend has a real auth API (POST /v1/auth/login, port 10010) issuing a cookie-based JWT session validated server-side. Financial data + cookie sessions + the template's BFF auth policy make BFF the right fit; RBAC (Importer vs Approver) is enforced by the frontend, which the BFF supports.

dataSource.note: OpenAPI specs are provided (frontend/docs/auth-api.yaml, transactions-api.yaml) and a sample CSV. Backend runs as two services: Auth API http://localhost:10010, Transaction Management API http://localhost:10005/transactions-api. Data service may not be running; build against mock data from the specs unless the backend is up.

compliance.note: No regulated/PII compliance domain called out in the brief. Confirm if that's wrong.

styling.note: Use the provided generic-financial design system for colours/fonts/spacing.

intakeApproval.note: Approve the project facts + epic plan once shown.

storyApproval.note: Approve the story list once shown.

perPartChecks: Importer can upload a file and see its transactions
Approver can approve a transaction and the status changes to Approved
Importer cannot see the approve/reject actions
handsOnApproval.note: Default trio (editable). These are the user-observable checks ticked at MANUAL-TEST.

mergeApproval.note: Approve the merge into main once EPIC-END is green.
